sections.title: Is Orlando Safe for Tourists in 2026?
sections.summary: Short answer: yes. Orlando's tourist zones — Disney, Universal, International Drive, and Lake Buena Vista — are among the safest in Florida. For most visitors the real risks are the heat, driving on busy highways, and a few tourist-targeted scams, not violent crime.
bullets: Orlando is one of the most visited cities in the world, built around heavily staffed, well-lit resort areas. For families and solo travelers alike, the tourist corridors are very safe — but Orlando is spread out and car-centric, so plan how you'll get around after dark.

This guide skips the scare stories and works through what visitors actually face — the Florida heat, parking lots at night, highway driving, and timeshare or ticket scams — with a clear plan for each.
Whether you're at a park, on International Drive, or out in downtown Orlando, it comes down to the same thing: stay aware, use rideshare at night, and know what to do if something feels off.
